chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّ الْأَرْضَ كُلَّهَا لِلْإِمَامِ ( عليه السلام )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 105 – The earth, all of it, is for the Imam<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (350/ 4)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (350/ 4)

parts.text: مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ
يُونُسَ بْنِ ظَبْيَانَ أَوِ الْمُعَلَّى بْنِ خُنَيْسٍ

text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا لَكُمْ مِنْ هَذِهِ الْأَرْضِ فَتَبَسَّمَ ثُمَّ قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى بَعَثَ جَبْرَئِيلَ ( عليه السلام ) وَ أَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَخْرِقَ بِإِبْهَامِهِ ثَمَانِيَةَ أَنْهَارٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ مِنْهَا سَيْحَانُ وَ جَيْحَانُ وَ هُوَ نَهَرُ بَلْخَ وَ الْخشوع وَ هُوَ نَهَرُ الشَّاشِ وَ مِهْرَانُ وَ هُوَ نَهَرُ الْهِنْدِ وَ نِيلُ مِصْرَ وَ دِجْلَةُ وَ الْفُرَاتُ

فَمَا سَقَتْ أَوِ اسْتَقَتْ فَهُوَ لَنَا وَ مَا كَانَ لَنَا فَهُوَ لِشِيعَتِنَا وَ لَيْسَ لِعَدُوِّنَا مِنْهُ شَيْ‏ءٌ إِلَّا مَا غَصَبَ عَلَيْهِ وَ إِنَّ وَلِيَّنَا لَفِي أَوْسَعَ فِيمَا بَيْنَ ذِهْ إِلَى ذِهْ يَعْنِي بَيْنَ السَّمَاءِ وَ الْأَرْضِ
ثُمَّ تَلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ قُلْ هِيَ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا الْمَغْصُوبِينَ عَلَيْهَا خالِصَةً لَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ بِلَا غَصْبٍ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Muhammad Bin Abdullah Bin Ahmad, from Ali Bin Al Numan, from Salih Bin Hamza, from Aban Bin Mus’ab, from Yunus Bin Zabyan, or al Moalla Bin Khunays who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>, ‘What have you all (Imams<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup>) to do from the earth?’ So he<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> smiled, then said: ‘Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Blessed and High Sent Jibraeel<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup> and Commanded him<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup> that he<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup> furrows eight rivers by his<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup> big toe in the earth – from these is Sayhan, and Jayhan, and it is the river Balkh, and Al-Khashou, and it is River Shash, and Mihran, and it is a river of India, and Nile of Egypt, and Dajla, and Euphrates.

So whatever is watered from or drawn, so it is for us<sup>asws</sup>, and whatever was for us<sup>asws</sup> so it is for our<sup>asws</sup> Shias, and there isn’t anything from it for our<sup>asws</sup> enemies except what they have usurped upon, and that our<sup>asws</sup> friends would be in (a place) more capacious in what is between this to this, meaning between the sky and the earth’.
Then heasws recited this Verse [7:32] Say: These are for the Believers in the life of this world the ones usurped upon especially on the Day of Judgement without being usurped’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Abdallah ibn Ahmad from Ali ibn al-Ni‘man from Salih ibn Hamza from Aban ibn Mus‘ab from Yunus ibn Zabyan or al-Mu‘alla ibn al-Khnith who has said the following. “I asked abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.), ‘What is for you from this earth?” He smiled and then said, “Allah, the Most Holy, the Most High, sent Jibril and commanded him to dig with his toe eight rivers on earth. Of these are the River Sayhan and Jayhan which is the River in Balkh (central Asia) the River al-Khshu‘ which is the River al-Shash, river Mihran which is India, River Nile in Egypt, Tigris and Euphrates. All that these rivers provide water or all that take water from them belong to us and whatever is for us is for our Shi‘a. There is nothing for our enemies except what they have usurped. Our friends and supporters in an place bigger than this and this, the sky and earth. The Imam (a.s.) then recited the words of Allah. “They are made for the believers in this world (but others have usurped it from them) and are exclusively for them in the life hereafter (no one would usurp from them).” [7:32]
